# Resonance 360

> Subspecialty MSK and interventional radiology practice in Singapore, led by Dr Tham Seng Choe (MBBS NUS, FRCR UK, AMIA Clinical Informatics). Offers expert MRI second opinions, image-guided pain interventions, and clinical AI advisory. Based at Mount Elizabeth Novena Hospital.

Resonance 360 is the practice of Dr Tham Seng Choe, a Senior Consultant Radiologist with 20+ years of subspecialty experience in musculoskeletal (MSK) and interventional radiology. The practice has two arms: (1) clinical services — MRI second opinions for sports, spine, joint, and soft-tissue cases, plus image-guided injections, nerve blocks, and spinal interventions; and (2) AI & innovation — advisory work bridging healthcare systems and AI companies on workflow redesign, radiology AI, and medical report optimisation. Second opinions are open to both referring clinicians and patients seeking a direct specialist read. Face-to-face consultations are not part of the second-opinion flow — the process is email enquiry → suitability review → scan submission (digital link preferred) → written subspecialty report. Located at the Radiologic Clinic, Mount Elizabeth Novena Hospital, Singapore.

## Core pages
- [Home](https://www.resonance360.com/): Overview of the practice and the two service lines.
- [About Dr Tham](https://www.resonance360.com/about.html): Background, credentials, training (NUS, UCSF, OHSU), and society affiliations including RADII (Singapore Radiological Society) Executive Committee.
- [Clinical Services](https://www.resonance360.com/clinical.html): MSK radiology second opinions and image-guided MSK/spine interventions. Full list of conditions covered and procedures offered.
- [AI & Innovation](https://www.resonance360.com/ai-innovation.html): Clinical AI advisory, workflow redesign, and medical report optimisation work; selected AI/research projects.
- [Insights](https://www.resonance360.com/insights.html): Writing by Dr Tham on AI in healthcare, radiology governance, and private practice — including the "Throw Punches" position paper (SSRN, 2026).
- [FAQ](https://www.resonance360.com/faq.html): Common questions about second opinions, scan submission, interventions, and the referral process.
- [Contact](https://www.resonance360.com/contact.html): Enquiry form and direct email for clinical or partnership enquiries.

## Key facts
- **Practitioner**: Dr Tham Seng Choe, Senior Consultant Radiologist
- **Credentials**: MBBS (NUS), FRCR (UK), AMIA Clinical Informatics (OHSU)
- **Subspecialty**: Musculoskeletal and interventional radiology
- **Location**: Radiologic Clinic, Mount Elizabeth Novena Hospital, Singapore
- **Clinical enquiries**: dr.tham@resonance360.com
- **General enquiries**: hello@resonance360.com
- **Languages**: English, Mandarin, Cantonese
- **LinkedIn**: https://www.linkedin.com/in/sengchoetham/
- **Publications**: peer-reviewed research in Skeletal Radiology and Arthroscopy; textbook contributions (Thieme); journal reviewer for Skeletal Radiology; "Throw Punches" (SSRN preprint, 2026)

## Services at a glance
- **MRI / CT / ultrasound second opinions** — spine, shoulder, hip, knee, ankle/foot, wrist/hand, elbow, soft tissue, sports and overuse injuries.
- **Image-guided interventions** (referral-based) — joint injections, tendon/bursa injections, perineural injections, epidural and facet injections, medial branch blocks, selective nerve root blocks, sacroiliac injections, vertebral augmentation, RFA for osteoid osteoma, MSK biopsy.
- **Clinical AI advisory** — workflow design, AI model evaluation, medical report optimisation, healthcare-AI vendor partnerships.

## How to engage
- **Second opinions**: email dr.tham@resonance360.com with the clinical question; cases are reviewed for suitability before acceptance; scans are then submitted (digital link preferred, or physically to Radiologic Clinic, Mount Elizabeth Novena); a written subspecialty report is returned.
- **Interventions**: referral-based; refer via dr.tham@resonance360.com.
- **AI partnerships**: contact via hello@resonance360.com.
